definition: Europe's post-2022 shift from Russian pipeline gas (~70% EUR-denominated) to seaborne LNG (USD-denominated, spot-priced) created ~$40-50bn/year in new structural USD demand. TTF-EUR/USD correlation shifted from r=-0.15 to r=-0.45. This is a permanent structural change to EUR/USD dynamics, confirmed by the January 2025 cold snap (TTF +40%, EUR/USD -2.5%).
evidence: Pre-2022: ~70% Russian pipeline gas (EUR-denominated); post-2022: seaborne LNG (USD-denominated)
TTF-EUR/USD correlation shift from r=-0.15 to r=-0.45
Europe imported ~120-130 million tonnes of LNG in 2024 at ~$400-450/t, virtually all USD-denominated
Gas composition shift: pre-2022 ~70% Russian pipeline (EUR contracts), post-2022 ~55-60% LNG
Seasonal validation: January 2025 cold snap, TTF +40%, EUR/USD -2.5%
New LNG terminals with 20+ year useful lives confirm infrastructure lock-in
open_questions: How to isolate the LNG-specific FX magnitude from other concurrent EUR/USD drivers?
Will European LNG terminal buildout and long-term contracts reduce spot-price sensitivity?
Whether markets have already priced this structural shift
Isolating the LNG channel's magnitude from other EUR/USD drivers remains difficult
